what_it_does: Defines a React workshop asset library sidebar with searchable tabs for blocks, layouts, icons, components, and imagery, supporting click insertion and drag payloads, plus a global resolver that converts library payloads into section objects or layout actions.
observations: Uses global React hook aliases instead of imports.
Defines fixed tab metadata and a small placeholder imagery catalogue in file scope.
Builds tab contents from window.WS_BLOCKS, window.WS_layoutsForType, window.CV_ICONS, window.COMPONENT_GROUPS, and LIB_IMAGERY.
Uses a custom drag data type application/x-cv-library with JSON payloads.
Click and drag interactions share the same payload objects.
Renders separate tile variants for icons, imagery, and generic library items.
Exposes WS_resolveLibraryPayload and Library on window.
standouts: The library UI is registry-driven while keeping local fallback imagery slots.
The drag payload resolver is separated from the rendered panel.
Each asset type uses a small discriminated payload shape keyed by kind.
conventions: File relies on global browser objects and globally registered workshop data.
CSS naming is consistently prefixed with ws-library.
React component state is local to tab selection and search query.
Callbacks are invoked with optional chaining.
No module imports or exports are used; integration is through window assignment.
concerns: WS_resolveLibraryPayload assumes window.WS_BLOCKS exists for block and icon payloads, which can throw if the global is absent.
The icon resolver reads window.WS_BLOCKS.icons into def but does not use it.
Drag setup silently swallows all errors in startDrag.
Tabs expose role attributes but do not include associated panel ids or aria-controls in this file.
notes: This file is the workshop canvas asset picker and payload adapter for inserting or applying library items.
questions: Are window.WS_BLOCKS and other registries guaranteed to be initialized before this file's resolver is used?
Should imagery payload tone be preserved by the image section resolver or is it only for library tile display?
Is the unused def lookup in the icon payload resolver intentional?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it replaces an icon-only rail with a searchable, tabbed library that supports click insertion and drag-to-drop payloads.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make workshop asset insertion discoverable and flexible by exposing multiple asset categories through one searchable panel and one payload-resolution path.
novelty: Combines a React sidebar library, custom drag MIME payloads, and a global payload-to-section resolver in one file-local integration point.
contribution: Contributes a neutral asset-library substrate: searchable category tabs, typed library payloads, click and drag insertion behavior, placeholder imagery slots, and a resolver that turns library choices into workshop section or layout operations.
summary_for_embedding: React workshop Library sidebar that renders searchable block, layout, icon, component, and imagery tiles and exposes a resolver for turning typed library payloads into canvas sections or layout actions.
intention_for_embedding: Built to replace an icon rail with a richer asset insertion panel that supports both click-to-add and drag-to-drop workflows.

symbols.does: Renders the workshop asset library sidebar and manages tab/search state plus click and drag interactions.
symbols.description: Library is a React component that switches among Blocks, Layouts, Icons, Components, and Imagery tabs, computes visible items from globals or local catalogue data, and renders matching tile controls. It sends layout payloads to onApplyLayout and all other payloads to onInsertBlock, while also preparing draggable payload data for canvas drop targets.

symbols.does: Serializes a library payload into the drag event and configures a copy drag image.
symbols.description: startDrag writes the payload as JSON under application/x-cv-library and sets the drag operation to copy. It attempts to clone the source tile as a temporary drag image, then removes that clone asynchronously.

symbols.does: Routes clicked library payloads to either layout application or block insertion callbacks.
symbols.description: clickInsert treats layout payloads as layout operations and forwards their layout id to onApplyLayout. Other payload kinds are passed through to onInsertBlock for downstream resolution or insertion.

symbols.does: Converts an icon-style identifier into a display label with spaces and capitalized word starts.
symbols.description: titleCase coerces the input to a string, replaces hyphens and underscores with spaces, and uppercases word-initial characters. It is used when creating labels for icon payloads in the resolver.
